steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank requires a noun and a relative clause: "materials that Palma Rodriguez uses in his art." The relative clause is dependent and introduced by "that."

steps.title: Apply the rule about restrictive relative clauses
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "materials that Palma Rodriguez uses in his art" is a restrictive relative clause — it identifies WHICH materials (the ones he uses in his art, not other materials). Restrictive clauses take NO commas. The clause is followed by a verb phrase continuing the sentence, so no closing comma either.
  • Choice A — "materials, that...": comma before "that" wrongly treats the clause as parenthetical; "that"-clauses are always restrictive and never take a preceding comma. ✗
  • Choice B — "materials that... his art": no commas — correctly treats the clause as restrictive. ✓
  • Choice C — "materials that... his art,": trailing comma incorrectly separates the clause from what follows. ✗
  • Choice D — "materials, that... his art,": both errors — wrong opening comma AND wrong closing comma. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
